UNDER APPLICATION TO THE CLERK FOR JUDGMENT UNDER SEC 585(1) C.C.P., IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF CHAN, ELMER TEP RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT AFEWERK, SENAIT INDIVIDUALLY AND JOINTLY DBA NICK'S FOOD KIDANE, ABREHA INDIVIDUALLY AND JOINTLY DBA NICK'S FOOD $78,147.49 PRINCIPAL, $541.69 INTEREST, $410.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $79,099.18, SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT

LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS SENAIT AFEWERK, ABREHA KIDANE'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENYED IN PART. THE MOTION TO SET ASIDE JUDGMENT AS TO DEFENDANT ABREHA KIDANE IS UNOPPOSED (AND ACTUALLY REQUESTED BY) THE LABOR COMMISSIONER AND THEREFORE IS GRANTED. THE MOTION TO SET ASIDE JUDGMENT AS TO DEFENDANT SENAIT AFEWERK IS DENIED. CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 473 DOES NOT APPLY IN THESE CIRCUMSTANCES. SEE PRESSLER V. DONALD BREN CO. (1982) 32 CAL. 3D 831. IN EFFECT, DEFENDANT AFEWERK IS ASKING THE COURT TO SET ASIDE A JUDGMENT SO THAT IT CAN TAKE A TIMELY APPEAL FROM THE LABOR COMMISSIONER'S DECISION AND HAVE A DE NOVO TRIAL TO THIS COURT. THIS IS NOT PERMITTED UNDER THE REASONING OF PRESSLER. THE LABOR COMMISSIONER IS ORDERED TO SERVE ANOTHER COPY OF ODA ON MR. KIDANE. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reinstate Judgment Entered September 6, 2012 And Set Aside Stay Of Enforcement Of The Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-03-2015 Granted Per Written Order

Appearances:
Fred Keeperman, Attorney of Law, Tel. 925.631.7710, for plaintiffs;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: ASSIGNEE THE NATIONAL COLLECTION AGENCY, INC.'S Motion To Reinstate Judgment Entered September 6, 2012 And Set Aside Stay Of Enforcement Of The Judgment is granted. No opposition filed. Order signed in open court. Judge Harold Kahn; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Post Undertaking
Off Calendar Aug-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. ASSIGNEE THE NATIONAL COLLECTION AGENCY, INC.'S Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Post Undertaking is Off calendar. Calendared in error. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

App To Reinstate Clerk'S Judgmnt On Final Order; Noticer
Off Calendar Jul-10-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: ATTY FRED KEEPERMAN, 925.631.7710, APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF ELMER TEP CHAN;
ATTY JOHN BROWN, 415.409.8600, APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT KIDANE.

No argument held. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. STATE LABOR COMMISSIONER'S Application To Reinstate Clerk's Judgment On Final Order is Denied. On May 17, 2013, State Labor Commissioner mail served the Order, Decision and Award. On May 25, 2013, Mr. Kidane timely filed with the Court a Notice of Appeal in case no. 531654. (Lab. Code 98.2(a).) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185.

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ELLIOT BECKELMAN, ATTORNEY, DEPARTMENT OF LABOR RELATIONS, 415.703.4877, FOR PLAINTIFF ELMER TEP CHAN.
ABREHA KEDANE AND SENAIT AFEWERK, DEFENDANTS IN PRO PER.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS SENAIT AFEWERK, ABREHA KIDANE'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT IS grantED in part and denyED in part. The motion to set aside judgment as to Defendant Abreha Kidane is unopposed (and actually requested by) the Labor Commissioner and therefore is granted. The motion to set aside judgment as to Defendant Senait Afewerk is denied. Code of Civil Procedure section 473 does not apply in these circumstances. See Pressler v. Donald Bren Co. (1982) 32 Cal. 3d 831. In effect, Defendant Afewerk is asking the court to set aside a judgment so that it can take a timely appeal from the Labor Commissioner's decision and have a de novo trial to this Court. This is not permitted under the reasoning of Pressler. THE LABOR COMMISSIONER IS ORDERED TO SERVE ANOTHER COPY OF ODA ON MR. KIDANE. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
